headline: PRESIDENT OF INDIA ADDRESSES INDIA-NORTH MACEDONIA BUSINESS FORUM
fullText:
President's Secretariat

PRESIDENT OF INDIA ADDRESSES INDIA-NORTH MACEDONIA BUSINESS FORUM

INDIA-NORTH MACEDONIA ECONOMIC AND COMMERCIAL RELATIONSHIP IS A FRONTIER WAITING TO BE FULLY CONQUERED: PRESIDENT DROUPADI MURMU

BUSINESSES FROM BOTH SIDES SHOULD TAKE THE LEAD IN IDENTIFYING VIABLE PROJECTS AND BUILDING LONG-TERM COMMERCIAL RELATIONSHIPS: PRESIDENT DROUPADI MURMU

21 JUL 2026 10:16PM by PIB Delhi

The President of India, Smt Droupadi Murmu delivered the inaugural address at the India-North Macedonia Business Forum at Skopje today (July 21, 2026). The President of the Republic of North Macedonia, H.E. Dr. Gordana Siljanovska-Davkova, members of the Government of North Macedonia, business leaders from India and North Macedonia and representatives of Industry Associations of the two countries were present on the occasion.

Addressing the gathering, the President said that this is the first visit by a President of India to North Macedonia, which shows the keen interest of India to engage with North Macedonia in all areas of mutual interest. She underlined that India-North Macedonia economic and commercial relationship is a frontier waiting to be fully conquered. The bilateral trade volume shows a steady positive trajectory, but it represents only a fraction of what is possible.

The President said that to move from trade to a true economic partnership, we must focus on mutual investments. She emphasized that the scope for new investments spans across various sectors, including digital innovation and artificial intelligence, pharmaceuticals and healthcare, agro-processing, and the green energy transition. She invited North Macedonia to participate in the World Audio Visual and Entertainment Summit (WAVES) to be held next year in India.

The President highlighted the need to encourage partnerships among start-ups, universities, research institutions and technology enterprises. She said that cooperation in Artificial Intelligence, cyber security and digital innovation should also be explored.

The President said that businesses from both sides should take lead in identifying viable projects and building long-term commercial relationships. She urged Chambers of Commerce, Investment agencies and Business Associations of both countries to establish linkages and facilitate exchanges of business delegations. She also urged companies to look beyond immediate transactions and invest in partnerships based on technology transfer, joint ventures, local value creation and employment generation. She expressed confidence that this Forum will generate new ideas and open new avenues of cooperation between India and North Macedonia.

- **Historical Significance**: First presidential visit by India to North Macedonia.  
- **Trade Status**: Bilateral trade shows a steady positive trajectory but remains a small fraction of potential.  
- **Investment Focus Areas**: Digital innovation & AI, pharmaceuticals & healthcare, agro‑processing, green energy transition.  
- **Event Invitation**: North Macedonia invited to participate in the World Audio Visual and Entertainment Summit (WAVES) to be held in India next year.

headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to Rules-Based Multilateral Trading System at WTO Trade Policy Review in Geneva
fullText:
Ministry of Commerce & Industry

India Reaffirms Commitment to Rules-Based Multilateral Trading System at WTO Trade Policy Review in Geneva

Commerce Secretary Highlights India's Strong Economic Performance and Record USD 863.1 Billion Exports at WTO Trade Policy Review

65 WTO Members Participate in India's Trade Policy Review, Highlight Trade Reforms and Economic Resilience

21 JUL 2026 10:13PM by PIB Delhi

India's 8th Trade Policy Review (TPR) at the World Trade Organization (WTO) commenced today in Geneva, with Commerce Secretary Shri Rajesh Agarwal leading the Indian delegation and reaffirming the country's commitment to a transparent, rules-based, inclusive and development-oriented multilateral trading system. The Trade Policy Review meeting, being held on 21 and 23 July 2026, provides WTO Members an opportunity to assess India's trade and related policies during the review period from 1 January 2021 to 31 December 2025.

In his opening statement, the Commerce Secretary highlighted India's strong economic performance, with the country recording an average annual growth of nearly 8 per cent during the review period despite an exceptionally challenging global environment. He noted that India's exports reached a record USD 863.1 billion in 2025-26 and emphasised that India is undertaking the unprecedented task of advancing the prosperity of nearly one-fifth of humanity amid global uncertainty, technological disruption, supply chain realignments and rising protectionism.

Shri Agarwal underlined that Aatmanirbhar Bharat is strengthening domestic capabilities while integrating India more deeply into global value chains. He highlighted key reforms undertaken through the Jan Vishwas Initiative, trade facilitation measures, digital customs, logistics modernisation, quality infrastructure, Digital Public Infrastructure, artificial intelligence, innovation, sustainability, agricultural resilience, expanding regional trade agreements, and inclusive development through South-South Cooperation and the Indian Technical and Economic Cooperation (ITEC) Programme. He also reaffirmed India's support for balanced, development-oriented WTO reforms that preserve and strengthen the multilateral trading system.

In her opening remarks, Trade Policy Review Body (TPRB) Chair Ambassador Nelle Pepe Tavita Levy welcomed the Indian delegation and highlighted India's remarkable growth as one of the fastest-growing major economies progressing towards the vision of Viksit Bharat by 2047. She recognised India's leadership in digitally delivered services exports and Digital Public Infrastructure reforms that have strengthened financial inclusion and trade facilitation. She also welcomed India's acceptance of the WTO Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies, marked by the deposition of its Instrument of Acceptance the previous day, and appreciated India's timely responses to all advance written questions, describing them as a demonstration of transparency under the Trade Policy Review Mechanism.

The discussant, H.E. Mr. Guilherme de Aguiar Patriota of Brazil, participating in his personal capacity, commended India's structural reforms, particularly the Goods and Services Tax (GST) for unifying the national market, digital trade reforms, expanding export strategy and recent regional trade agreements. He also appreciated India's Duty-Free Tariff Preference Scheme for Least Developed Countries (LDCs) and its longstanding contribution to the WTO amid evolving multilateral challenges. He observed that India's Digital Public Infrastructure and development-oriented reforms offer valuable lessons for developing countries and LDCs pursuing digital transformation.

During the meeting, 65 WTO Members are participating in the discussion on various aspects of India's trade policy. Several Members recalled the value of their trade and economic relationship with India and broadly appreciated the country's constructive engagement in the multilateral trading system and its strong economic resilience. Members also highlighted India's achievements in Digital Public Infrastructure, customs and trade facilitation modernisation, innovation and startup initiatives, expanding regional trade agreements, and efforts to integrate MSMEs into global value chains. They also welcomed India's acceptance of the WTO Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies.

Several Members sought further clarifications on issues relating to trade and investment regimes, certain trade policy measures and practices, Quality Control Orders (QCOs), trade remedy measures and matters relating to ongoing WTO reforms.

Alongside the Trade Policy Review, India organised an exhibition showcasing a diverse range of Indian products, highlighting the country's craftsmanship, innovation and quality. Traditional papier-mâché coasters presented as souvenirs were warmly appreciated by WTO delegates as a reflection of India's rich artisanal heritage.

India's Trade Policy Review meeting will conclude on 23 July 2026, following further discussions among WTO Members and India's responses to the issues raised during the review.

Abhishek Dayal/ Abhijith Narayanan/ Ishita Biswas

(Release ID: 2287487)
summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
The 8th World Trade Organization Trade Policy Review convened in Geneva from 21 to 23 July 2026, led by Indian Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agarwal, reaffirming India’s commitment to a transparent, rules‑based multilateral trading system and highlighting its record‑high exports and growth during the review period.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
- 8th Trade Policy Review (TPR) held on 21 and 23 July 2026 in Geneva; 65 WTO members participated.  
- Review period: 1 January 2021 – 31 December 2025.  
- Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agarwal led the Indian delegation.  
- India recorded a record USD 863.1 billion in exports for FY 2025‑26.  
- Average annual economic growth of nearly 8 % during the review period.  
- India accepted the WTO Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies, depositing its Instrument of Acceptance the day before the meeting.  
- Highlighted reforms: Jan Vishwas Initiative, trade facilitation, digital customs, logistics modernisation, quality infrastructure, Digital Public Infrastructure, AI, innovation, sustainability, agricultural resilience, regional trade agreements, South‑South Cooperation, and ITEC Programme.  
- Discussant from Brazil praised GST, digital trade reforms, export strategy, regional agreements, Duty‑Free Tariff Preference Scheme for LDCs, and India’s contributions to WTO reforms.  
- Exhibition of Indian products, including traditional papier‑mâché coasters, showcased craftsmanship to WTO delegates.  
- Opening remarks by TPRB Chair Ambassador Nelle Pepe Tavita Levy recognized India’s fast‑growth and leadership in digitally delivered services and DPI reforms.

headline: Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law and Justice hosts 8th Meeting of BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services
fullText:
Ministry of Law and Justice

Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law and Justice hosts 8th Meeting of BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services

Meeting focused on the theme, Exchanging Experiences and Best Practices in Criminal Adjudication from the Prosecution’s Perspective

21 JUL 2026 9:00PM by PIB Delhi

The Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India, under the aegis of India’s 2026 BRICS Chairship, has successfully hosted the 8th Meeting of BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services in virtual mode from 20–21 July 2026. Presided over by the Ld. Attorney General for India, Shri R. Venkataramani, this important official dialogue brought together Heads of Prosecution Services, senior officials, delegates, and legal leadership from Brazil, China, Egypt, Ethiopia, Indonesia, Iran, Russia, South Africa and UAE. Inaugurating the session, Shri R. Venkataramani delivered the welcome address, emphasizing "the rule of law, the fair and timely administration of criminal justice, and mutual trust between our prosecutorial institutions are, in many respects, the quieter foundations on which the resilience and sustainability that this year's Chairship seeks to build ultimately rest." The landmark meeting perfectly advanced India's overarching BRICS vision of “Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability.” By uniting the prosecution leadership of member countries, the meeting highlighted a shared commitment to fostering stable, fair, and predictable legal frameworks that support economic growth across the entire BRICS bloc.

The virtual meeting focused on the theme, “Exchanging Experiences and Best Practices in Criminal Adjudication from the Prosecution’s Perspective”. Over the two days, the BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services discussed modern institutional challenges and exchanged vital practices of adjudication. The deliberations carried forward the digital thread initiated in previous forums, moving the concepts of Artificial Intelligence and justice into practical, day-to-day procedures. By examining technology-enabled adjudication, the meeting addressed essential areas such as the digitisation of criminal procedures, the management of electronic evidence, and the implementation of time-bound trials.

A central focus of the dialogue was the shared goal of making the criminal justice system more sensitive, transparent, and victim-centric. Member countries shared their unique experiences to improve prosecutorial effectiveness and build greater public trust in prosecution services. Recognizing that tackling complex modern challenges such as transnational financial crimes, cyber threats, corruption, and environmental violations requires strong cross-border cooperation, delegates deliberated on key mechanisms. These included developing a BRICS Model Framework for Judicial Cooperation to reduce delay and improve evidence sharing, as well as leveraging platforms like India’s National Forensic Sciences University (NFSU) as collaborative global platforms for legal research, capacity building, and knowledge exchange among prosecution services.

(Release ID: 2287430)

summaryText:

The Department of Legal Affairs, Ministry of Law and Justice hosted the 8th Meeting of BRICS Heads of Prosecution Services as part of India’s 2026 BRICS Chairship, held virtually on 20‑21 July 2026 to exchange experiences and best practices in criminal adjudication.

headline: PRESIDENT OF INDIA ADDRESSES THE ASSEMBLY OF NORTH MACEDONIA
fullText:
President's Secretariat

PRESIDENT OF INDIA ADDRESSES THE ASSEMBLY OF NORTH MACEDONIA

INTERACTIONS BETWEEN OUR MEMBERS OF PARLIAMENT BUILD INSTITUTIONAL TRUST THAT ENDURES BEYOND POLITICAL CYCLES: PRESIDENT DROUPADI MURMU

INDIA IS COMMITTED TO SCALING UP ITS ENGAGEMENT BILATERALLY AND ACROSS THE WIDER REGION: PRESIDENT DROUPADI MURMU

21 JUL 2026 8:53PM by PIB Delhi

The President of India, Smt Droupadi Murmu addressed the members of the Assembly of North Macedonia at Skopje today (July 21, 2026) in the presence of the President of North Macedonia, H.E. Dr. Gordana Siljanovska-Davkova.

Speaking on the occasion, the President said that we in India regard the Republic of North Macedonia not as a distant country but as an important pillar in the architecture of a stable, multipolar, and prosperous global future. Our relationship was set in motion more than two millennia ago. The historic expeditions of warriors and traders from this region opened vital corridors for trade, philosophy, and art, connecting our early civilisations.

The President said that democracy is not merely a system of governance; it is a shared article of faith that unites India and North Macedonia. Our parliaments are the guardians of our peoples' freedoms, the architects of our laws, and the mirrors of our national conscience. This is why parliamentary diplomacy is important. She stated that interactions between our Members of Parliament build institutional trust that endures beyond political cycles. She urged young legislators to travel between New Delhi and Skopje and share best practices in parliamentary practices and public policy formulation.

The President underlined that the geopolitical and economic centres of gravity are shifting. In this new era, India views Southeast Europe not as a region on the margins but as a dynamic theatre of growth and innovation. She said that North Macedonia sits at the crossroads of this transformation. It is a vital gateway connecting the Mediterranean with Central Europe, and East with West. She stated that India is committed to scaling up its engagement bilaterally and across the wider region, including through emerging trade and investment opportunities.

The President underlined that no nation can prosper in isolation. She highlighted that India’s ancient civilisational philosophy is rooted in the ancient dictum -Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam - the world is one family. When the global landscape is fractured by conflict and economic division, this philosophy calls for building bridges, not walls. She stated that together, as two democratic nations anchored in history and driven by a vision for tomorrow, we should chart a course that brings peace and prosperity to our peoples.

The Minister of State for Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution Smt. Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya, and Members of Parliament Dr. Dinesh Sharma and Shri Vijay Baghel were also present on the occasion.

Please click here to see the President's Speech

(Release ID: 2287423)

summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
President of India Smt Droupadi Murmu addressed the Assembly of North Macedonia in Skopje on July 21, 2026, emphasizing shared democratic values, historical ties, and India’s commitment to strengthening bilateral and regional engagement. The event highlighted the role of parliamentary diplomacy in fostering institutional trust and cooperation between the two nations.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Date and Location**: Address held on July 21, 2026, in Skopje, North Macedonia.  
- **Attendees**: President of India Smt Droupadi Murmu, President of North Macedonia H.E. Dr. Gordana Siljanovska-Davkova, Minister of State for Consumer Affairs Smt. Nimuben Jayantibhai Bambhaniya, and MPs Dr. Dinesh Sharma and Shri Vijay Baghel.  
- **Historical Context**: India and North Macedonia share ancient trade, philosophical, and artistic links dating back over two millennia.  
- **Democratic Values**: Democracy was described as a "shared article of faith" and parliamentary diplomacy was highlighted as critical for enduring institutional trust.  
- **Geopolitical Vision**: India views Southeast Europe as a "dynamic theatre of growth," with North Macedonia positioned as a strategic gateway between the Mediterranean and Central Europe.  
- **Commitment to Engagement**: India pledged to scale up bilateral and regional cooperation through trade and investment opportunities.  
- **Philosophical Foundation**: India’s "Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam" principle (the world is one family) was cited as a call for global unity amid conflict and division.  
- **Call to Action**: Encouragement for young legislators to collaborate on parliamentary practices and public policy.

headline: NCVET Recognises GD Goenka University as Awarding Body (AB–Standard)
fullText:
Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

NCVET Recognises GD Goenka University as Awarding Body (AB–Standard)

21 JUL 2026 7:48PM by PIB Delhi

National Council for Vocational Education and Training (NCVET) has signed agreement with GD Goenka University for NCVET recognition as an Awarding Body (AB–Standard) to GD Goenka University.

The agreement signing ceremony was chaired by Shri Niranjan Kumar Sudhansu, Additional Secretary, Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship and Executive Member, NCVET. The ceremony was also attended by Prof. Dinesh Shenoy, Vice Chancellor, GD Goenka University, Lt. Col. Vikram Singh Bhati, Director, Recognition, NCVET, and Lt Col Saurabh S Bisht, Director, Monitoring, NCVET

Through this recognition, GD Goenka University will be eligible to award and certify the learners where training is directly being imparted by the Awarding Body in campuses or training centers owned or fully managed by it for its approved or adopted qualifications.

GD Goenka University developed its first NCVET-approved qualification pack titled as "Mental Health Well-Being Facilitator". The aim of this module is to prepare learners to work in the domain of mental health, recognise common mental health concerns, deliver evidence‑informed short interventions and group facilitation, and refer or signpost individuals to appropriate support within the community.

(Release ID: 2287354)

headline: NCVET Recognises NITTTR Kolkata as Awarding Body (Dual)
fullText:
Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

NCVET Recognises NITTTR Kolkata as Awarding Body (Dual)

21 JUL 2026 7:45PM by PIB Delhi

National Council for Vocational Education and Training (NCVET) has recognised the National Institute of Technical Teachers’ Training and Research (NITTTR), Kolkata, Ministry of Education, Government of India, as an Awarding Body (Dual). With this recognition, NITTTR Kolkata will be eligible to award, assess and certify the learners where training is directly being imparted by the Awarding Body in campuses or training centers owned or fully managed by it for its approved or adopted qualifications.

The agreement signing ceremony was chaired by Shri Niranjan Kumar Sudhansu, Additional Secretary, Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship and Executive Member, NCVET. The ceremony was also attended by Lt. Col. Vikram Singh Bhati, Director, Recognition, NCVET; Lt Col Saurabh S Bisht, Director, Monitoring, NCVET; Prof. V.M.S.R. Murthy, Director, NITTTR Kolkata and Prof. Naveen BP, Professor & Head, CE, NITTTR Kolkata.

As a significant milestone, NCVET has approved the first qualification developed by NITTTR Kolkata titled “Fundamentals of Soil Testing.” This programme has been designed to provide participants with a strong foundation in soil investigation, laboratory testing procedures, interpretation of soil properties, quality control practices, and the application of Indian Standards relevant to geotechnical engineering.

The qualification aims to bridge the growing demand for skilled manpower in the construction, infrastructure, transportation, and geotechnical sectors by equipping learners with industry-relevant knowledge and practical skills. The programme also supports the national vision of strengthening technical and vocational education through competency-based training aligned with the National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF).

(Release ID: 2287349)

headline: NCVET Grants Awarding Body (Dual) Recognition to NIIT Foundation to Strengthen AI-Enabled Skill Development Across India
fullText:
Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

NCVET Grants Awarding Body (Dual) Recognition to NIIT Foundation to Strengthen AI-Enabled Skill Development Across India

21 JUL 2026 7:42PM by PIB Delhi

National Council for Vocational Education and Training (NCVET) signed agreement with NIIT Foundation, granting recognition to NIIT Foundation as an Awarding Body (Dual). With this recognition, NIIT Foundation will be eligible to directly conduct training, undertake assessments, and award NSQF-aligned certifications for approved qualifications across its pan-India network, in accordance with the regulatory framework of NCVET.

The agreement signing ceremony was chaired by Shri Niranjan Kumar Sudhansu, Additional Secretary, Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship and Executive Member, NCVET. The ceremony was also attended by Lt. Col. Vikram Singh Bhati, Director, Recognition, NCVET, Lt Col Saurabh S Bisht, Director, Monitoring, NCVET and Ms. Charu Kapoor, Country Director, NIIT Foundation.

As part of this recognition, NCVET has approved the first two qualifications developed by NIIT Foundation. The first qualification, Fundamentals of Digital Productivity with AI Tools (NSQF Level 3), equips learners with essential workplace productivity, digital collaboration, data handling, workplace readiness, and AI-enabled workflow management skills. The second qualification, Fundamentals of Data Management with AI Tools (NSQF Level 4), develops foundational and applied competencies in data handling, analysis, reporting, visualization, dashboard creation, and the effective use of AI-assisted productivity tools.

The recognition reflects NCVET's continued commitment to strengthening India's vocational education ecosystem by promoting innovative, industry-responsive qualifications aligned with emerging technologies and future workforce requirements.

(Release ID: 2287346)

fullText:
Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

NCVET recognises Central Hindi Training Institute (CHTI) as Awarding Body (Dual)

21 JUL 2026 7:41PM by PIB Delhi

National Council for Vocational Education and Training (NCVET) and the Central Hindi Training Institute (CHTI), Department of Official Language signed an agreement for NCVET Recognition as an Awarding Body (AB - Dual) to CHTI. Through this recognition, CHTI will be eligible to award, assess, and certify learners where training is directly being imparted by the Awarding Body in campuses or training centers owned or fully managed by it for its approved or adopted qualifications.

The agreement signing ceremony was chaired by Shri Niranjan Kumar Sudhansu, Additional Secretary, Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship and Executive Member, NCVET. The ceremony was also attended by Lt. Col. Vikram Singh Bhati, Director, Recognition, NCVET and Lt Col Saurabh S Bisht, Director, Monitoring, NCVET, Lt. Col Ramnaresh Sharma, Director, CHTI, Sh. Narender Kumar Prasad, Joint Director, Smt. Anita Raikwar, Deputy Director and sh. Charanjeet Verma, Advisor/A.D. CHTI.

CHTI, an institution under the Department of Official Language, was established to provide specialized in-service training in Hindi language proficiency, Hindi word processing and Hindi stenography. CHTI is now empowered to perform both independent assessment and certification functions under the National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF). The first qualification developed by CHTI, "Fundamentals of Hindi Word Processing & Hindi Stenography," has already obtained NCVET approval.

This initiative aligns with the government's vision to enhance linguistic skills, promote digital governance in the official language, and bridge the competency gap for administrative staff across central government institutions, public sector undertakings, nationalized banks and the general public.

headline: CAQM Reviews Enforcement Actions across NCR; 175 Inspections conducted in 14 days; 25 Resumption Orders issued after Compliance Verification
fullText:
Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

CAQM Reviews Enforcement Actions across NCR; 175 Inspections conducted in 14 days; 25 Resumption Orders issued after Compliance Verification

Ghaziabad Road Redevelopment found fully compliant with CAQM's Standard Framework

21 JUL 2026 7:23PM by PIB Delhi

The 135th meeting of the Enforcement Task Force (ETF) of the Commission for Air Quality Management (CAQM) in NCR and Adjoining Areas, held on 20.07.2026, reviewed enforcement actions, inspections and compliance status across the National Capital Region (NCR), for the reporting period from 01.07.2026 to 13.07.2026. During the 14-day period, the Commission's Flying Squads carried out 175 inspections covering 17 Construction and Demolition (C&D) sites, 90 industrial units and 68 Diesel Generator (DG) set-related inspections.

Based on reported violations, proposals have been made for closure of one project/industry, sealing of 19 DG sets, issuance of nine compliance orders/directions and imposition of Environmental Compensation (EC) in four cases.

The Enforcement Task Force also reviewed action taken on decisions of the 134th ETF meeting and noted that all actionable cases related to the industrial sector, C&D sector and DG sets have been acted upon by the concerned agencies. A total of 25 resumption orders have been issued after verification of compliance, including two in Delhi, 11 in Haryana, 10 in Uttar Pradesh and two in Rajasthan.

The meeting also reviewed the cumulative enforcement status, as on 20.07.2026. It noted that the Commission's Flying Squads have so far inspected 27,925 units, projects and entities. Based on these inspections, 1,803 closure directions have been issued, of which 1,446 resumption orders have been granted after verification of compliance. In addition, 126 cases have been transferred to the respective State Pollution Control Boards (SPCBs)/Delhi Pollution Control Committee (DPCC) for final decision, while 231 entities remain under examination for issuance of resumption orders.

The ETF further reviewed the status of road redevelopment works undertaken by Ghaziabad Nagar Nigam in accordance with the Commission's Standard Framework. The inspection covered 42 road stretches spanning approximately nine kilometres, comprising roads with a Right of Way (RoW) of less than 10 metres. All the inspected road stretches were found to have complete end-to-end paving, indicating full compliance with the Standard Framework. The Commission emphasised that adoption of the Standard Framework by all road-owning agencies across Delhi-NCR remains essential for long-term mitigation of road dust and directed that all ongoing and future road redevelopment works be undertaken in strict conformity with it.

The Commission reiterated the need to strengthen enforcement mechanisms, ensure timely verification of compliance, improve coordination among implementing agencies and strictly enforce prescribed norms for air pollution abatement across NCR. It also stressed the importance of enhancing inspection efficiency, ensuring accountability and expediting enforcement action in priority sectors, including industrial emissions, DG sets and C&D activities.

(Release ID: 2287330)

headline: Blue Economy of Odisha under PMMSY
fullText:
Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying

Blue Economy of Odisha under PMMSY

22 JUL 2026 1:36PM by PIB Delhi

The Department of Fisheries (DoF), Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying (MoFAH&D), Government of India, has been implementing the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) across all States and Union Territories, including Odisha, since 2020–21. During this period, the Department has approved fisheries development proposals submitted by the Government of Odisha, amounting to ₹1,301.18 crore, including a Central share of ₹484.64 crore, covering a wide range of interventions to strengthen the fisheries and aquaculture value chain. Under PMMSY, DoF, GoI, accorded approval in January 2025 for the establishment of the Integrated Aqua Park at Basantapur, Sambalpur with a project cost of ₹100 crore. As reported by the State Government, key preparatory works such as land procurement, issuance of the letter of intent to the executing agency, and construction of the approach road and boundary walls, have been completed. Construction of the in‑house hatchery facility, grow‑out ponds, processing units, research facilities and the effluent treatment plant are at various stage.

(b) The Department of Fisheries (DoF), Government of India, under the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY), has also approved the construction of the State of Art Wholesale Fish Market at Pandara, Bhubaneswar, at a total project cost of ₹59.13 crore, comprising 44 wholesale units and 99 retail units. The market is designed with modern sustainability features, including a renewable‑energy‑based power system and rainwater harvesting infrastructure, aimed at reducing dependence on conventional energy sources and improving resource efficiency. As reported by the Government of Odisha, land reclamation stage has been completed.

(c) and (d) The Government of India has extended the Kisan Credit Card (KCC) facility to fishers and fish farmers since FY 2018‑19, thereby ensuring timely and affordable working capital for fisheries‑related activities. The Department of Fisheries (DoF), Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying, in collaboration with the Department of Financial Services (DFS), Ministry of Finance, is actively engaging with all States/UTs and the State Level Bankers’ Committee (SLBC) to raise awareness and disseminate information among fishers and fish farmers for availing KCC benefits.

As per the Government of Odisha, the State Government conducts regular awareness programmes through Matsya O Pranee Sampad Melas and fishermen meetings at the district, block, and village levels to enhance uptake of the scheme. Further, the State Government provides an additional interest subvention of 4% for loans up to ₹1.00 lakh and 2% for loans above ₹1.00 lakh and up to ₹3.00 lakh. This makes KCC loans interest‑free up to ₹1.00 lakh and reduces the effective interest rate to 2% for loans between ₹1.00 lakh and ₹3.00 lakh, thereby significantly improving credit affordability for beneficiaries.

This Information was given by Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying Minister Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh alias Lalan Singh in the Rajya Sabha in response to a question asked.

(Release ID: 2287593)
summaryText:

The Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, as part of the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) initiative, has approved fisheries development projects worth ₹1,301.18 crore in Odisha, including the establishment of an Integrated Aqua Park and a State of Art Wholesale Fish Market.

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS
* The Department of Fisheries, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying, has approved fisheries development proposals submitted by the Government of Odisha, totaling ₹1,301.18 crore.

headline: Welfare of shrimp farmer
fullText:
Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying

Welfare of shrimp farmer

22 JUL 2026 1:36PM by PIB Delhi

The Department of Fisheries, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying has taken up several initiatives for holistic development of fisheries and aquaculture in the country including implementation of various schemes such as (i) Centrally Sponsored Scheme on Blue Revolution : Integrated Development and Management of Fisheries implemented for a period of 5 years from FY2015-16 to FY2019-20, (ii) Fisheries&Aquaculture Infrastructure Development Fund (FIDF) implemented with effect from year 2018-19, (iii) Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) and (iv) Pradhan Mantri Matsya Kisan Samridhi Sah Yojana (PMMKSSY). Additionally, the Government of India with effect from the financial year 2018-19 has extended the Kisan Credit Card (KCC) facility to fishers and fish famers to help them to meet their working capital requirements. The Department of Fisheries, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying during the last 3 years financial years (2023-24 to 2025-26) has accorded approvals to the fisheries and aquaculture development proposals of various State Governments and Union Territories (UTs) at a total cost of Rs.6,885.18 crore with central share of Rs.3,159.83 crore under the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY). The central funds of Rs.2,208.74 crore has been released during the last three years (2023-24 to 2025-26) to the State Governments and UTs for implementation of various approved fisheries and aquaculture development projects under PMMSY. State/UT-wise details of the proposals approved and central funds released during the last three financial years (2023-24 to 2025-26) under PMMSY are furnished at Annexure-I.

(c) and (d) : To address the challenges of rising input costs, disease outbreaks and fluctuations in international market prices, the Government of India has been implementing measures several measures such as promotion of biosecurity, disease surveillance and diagnostics, dissemination of best management practices, and capacity building of fish farmers. Under the National Surveillance Programme for Aquatic Animal Diseases (NSPAAD) implemented under PMMSY, regular surveillance, early detection, reporting, and monitoring of aquatic animal diseases are undertaken to facilitate timely disease management and minimize production losses. Further, under PMMSY financial assistance has also been provided for establishment and strengthening of aquatic animal health infrastructure, disease diagnostic laboratories, hatcheries, and other interventions aimed at improving the sustainability, resilience, and competitiveness of the shrimp farming sector.

The Department of Fisheries, Government of India under the Pradhan Mantri,Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) inter-alia provides social security to fishers through group accidental insurance coverage to both marine and inland fishers and allied fish workers wherein the entire insurance premium amount is shared between the Centre& State in the ratio of 60:40 basis, 90:10 for the Himalayan&North Eastern States and in the case of the Union Territories, the entire premium amount is paid by the Centre. The insurance coverage provided under the PMMSY includes (i) Rs.5,00,000/- against death or permanent total disability, (ii) Rs.2,50,000/- for permanent partial disability and (iii) hospitalization expenses in the event of accident for a sum of Rs.25,000/. During the last three years (2023-24 to 2025-26), the Department of Fisheries has released a sum of Rs.54.03 crore for insurance coverage of 103.73 lakh fishers with an average of 34.57 lakh fishers annually.

In addition, the Department of Fisheries, Government of India under the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Kisan Samridhi Sah-Yojana (PM-MKSSY) also provides one time incentive for purchase of aquaculture insurance by farmers. The insurance products available under this scheme includes: (i) Basic Insurance which covers yield losses due to non-preventable perils(risks), such as summer kill, pollution, earthquakes, cyclones, floods, other natural calamities, riots, malicious acts of third parties including poisoning, loss of crop due to structural damage of farms and (ii) Comprehensive Insurance which covers perils under Basic Insurance and additional perils due to diseases etc. The one time incentive for aquaculture insurance is provided at the rate of 40% of the premium paid with a ceiling up to ₹25,000 per hectare, or ₹1lakh per farmer for 4 hectares Water Spread Area (WSA). For the aquaculture system including intensive systems like farms, cage culture, RAS, bio-floc, and raceways etc., aquaculture insurance is provided at the rate of 40% of the premium paid with a ceiling up to ₹1 lakh per farmer for 1800 m3 for intensive aquaculture systems. Further, the SC/ST and women beneficiaries are provided an additional 10% of incentive.

headline: Development of cold water fisheries
fullText:
Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying

Development of cold water fisheries

22 JUL 2026 1:34PM by PIB Delhi

The Department of Fisheries, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying has been implementing a flagship scheme Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) with an investment of Rs 20,050 crore in all the States/Union Territories. In order to address the infrastructure gaps in fisheries and aquaculture, the Department of Fisheries, Government of India with effect from the financial year 2018-19 has also been implementing a dedicated fund Fisheries and Aquaculture Infrastructure Development Fund (FIDF) with a fund size of Rs 7522.48 crore.

The PMMSY inter alia aims to address critical gaps in fish production and productivity, quality, technology, post-harvest infrastructure and management, modernisation and strengthening of value chain, traceability, establishing a robust fisheries management framework and fishers’ welfare in cold water fisheries.

The Department of Fisheries, Government of India, has approved various fisheries developmental projects of Himalayan and Hilly States for an amount of Rs.706 crore including projects for cold water fisheries development. A framework titled Model Guidelines for the Development of Cold Water Fisheries was issued on 14th March, 2026 to support States in developing coldwater fisheries and its associated infrastructure. These guidelines provide specifications for development of Raceways, Re-circulatory Aquaculture Systems (RAS), hatcheries, feed mills, disease prevention and market linkagesfor augmenting production, value addition and marketing of commercially important coldwater fish species like Rainbow Trout, Golden Mahseer and Snow Trout. In addition, the Department of Fisheries, Government of India has notified four clusters specifically for development of cold water fisheries at Anant Nag District in Jammu and Kashmir, Pithoragarh District in Uttarakhand, Kargil District in Ladakh and Kullu District in Himachal Pradesh.

(c) and (d) : The Department of Fisheries, Government of India promotes adoption of emerging technologies such as RAS, Biofloc, Cages, IoT‑based water quality monitoring, GIS mapping, automated feeders and digital market platforms for fish farmers in remote and mountainous regions. Under PMMSY, a model RAS for coldwater fisheries has been developed through Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR)-Central Institute of Coldwater Fisheries Research (ICAR-CICFR), Bhimtal for intensive trout production and year‑round breeding of Golden Mahseer. Moreover, ICAR‑Central Institute of Fisheries Technology (CIFT) Kochi has supported fish processing and Ready‑to‑Eat facilities in Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand.

This Information was given by Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying Minister Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh alias Lalan Singh in the Rajya Sabha in response to a question asked.

Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying

Allocation of funds under Blue Revolution

22 JUL 2026 1:33PM by PIB Delhi

The Department of Fisheries, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying, Government of India with effect from the Financial Year 2020-21 has been implementing a flagship scheme of the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) for holistic development of fisheries and aquaculture sector in the country. The State/UT-wise details of fund approved and released under the PMMSY during the last three financial years (2023-24 to 2025-26) is furnished at Annexure-I. The State/UT-wise allocation of central funds under PMMSY are made on annual basis depending on the overall budgetary allocation, performance of the States/UTs, fund absorbing capacity, physical and financial progress, liabilities created in respect of the projects approved in the previous financial years. There is no disparity in allocation of funds among the States and Union Territories under the PMMSY. The fisheries and aquaculture development projects and initiatives taken by the Department of Fisheries, Government of India under the PMMSY have contributed towards augmentation of fish production, productivity and exports. During the last five years (2020-21 to 2024-25), fish production in the country has increased from 141.64 lakh tonne in 2019-20 to 197.75 lakh tonne in 2024-25 (39.61% increase) and fisheries export from Rs.46,666 crore (2019-20) to Rs.73,890 crore (2025-26) (58.34% increase). The Department of Fisheries has developed the PMMSY Management Information System (MIS) as a centralized digital portal and dashboard utilized to track the progress of the Fisheries projects taken up under Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY). The MIS aggregates district and state-level data to enable monitoring and evaluation of the progress. Issuance of need based advisories, regular physical and virtual meetings with the States/UTs and field visits are undertaken to monitor and review the progress of projects and improve the effective utilization of funds.

headline: Women led cooperatives in fisheries
fullText:
Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying

Women led cooperatives in fisheries

22 JUL 2026 1:32PM by PIB Delhi

The Department of Fisheries, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying under the ongoing Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) supports the formation of Fisheries Cooperative Societies across the country including in the state of Andhra Pradesh. The Government of Andhra Pradesh has informed that a total of 213 women-led coastal Cooperative Societies have been formed in the areas of fisheries, seaweed cultivation and marine processing, benefitting 27,447 beneficiaries across 12 districts, including Kakinada from 1st April 2021 to 15th June 2026 . The district wise details of women-led coastal cooperative societies along with the number of beneficiaries associated with them, are given at Annexure-I.

(c) For the promotion and strengthening of women-led coastal fisheries cooperatives across the country, the Government of India is continuously engaging with the States and Union Territories through various schemes and programmes. As part of these efforts, fisheries cooperatives are encouraged to register on the National Fisheries Digital Platform (NFDP) and are eligible for higher incentives, including performance grants of up to ₹2 crore under the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Kisan Samridhi Sah-Yojana (PMMKSSY). Women-led fisheries cooperatives are also accorded priority in the issuance of Access Passes for fishing in the Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) and Letters of Authorization (LoA) for High Seas fishing. Further, to facilitate greater participation of fisheries cooperatives in High Seas fishing, the application and renewal fee for the Letter of Authorization has been reduced from ₹25,000 to ₹5,000.

(d) As a part of Blue Economy, the Department of Fisheries, through National Fisheries Development Board (NFDB), has formulated an action plan to support the formation of 12,000 fisheries cooperatives across the country, including women-led Coastal Cooperative Societies in uncovered panchayats and villages. In addition, the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Kisan Samridhi Saha-Yojana (PM-MKSSY) a sub-scheme of PMMSY envisages for strengthening of 5500 Primary Fisheries Cooperative Societies across the country including women-led Fisheries Cooperative Societies, with mentoring, capacity building and need based financial support.

headline: Traceability systems in seafood export
fullText:
Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying

Traceability systems in seafood export

22 JUL 2026 1:30PM by PIB Delhi

The Department of Fisheries, Government of India, is implementing several strategic initiatives to strengthen seafood export infrastructure under the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY). Under PMMSY, projects for the development of the fisheries and aquaculture sector, amounting to ₹21,394.88 crore with a Central share of ₹9,510.89 crore, have been approved, including those for the creation and strengthening of export infrastructure. Further, infrastructure has been augmented through the Fisheries and Aquaculture Infrastructure Development Fund (FIDF), under which 18 major projects, with a total project cost of ₹345.89 crore, have been supported, primarily for the expansion and modernization of fish processing plants. The year-wise and State-wise details of financial assistance provided under PMMSY and FIDF are enclosed at Annexure-II and Annexure-III, respectively. In addition, the MPEDA is implementing the Technology Development for Specific Value-Added Marine Products (TDSVMP) Scheme, under which financial assistance is provided for the development of value-added infrastructure. The Scheme supports the establishment and modernization of fish processing facilities, cold storage, automated processing machinery, infrastructure for handling live, chilled, ornamental, and dried fish, and the development of new value-added seafood products. During the period from 2021-22 to 2025-26, MPEDA has provided financial assistance of ₹118.10 crore to 89 seafood processing units for infrastructure development under the Scheme.

The Export Inspection Council (EIC), under the Department of Commerce, is the Competent Authority for quality control and inspection of notified export products, including marine products. To ensure compliance with importing countries' requirements, EIC conducts periodic training on Food Safety Management System (FSMS) requirements for approved seafood export establishments and mandates testing of aquaculture shrimp for banned antibiotics during export certification. The MPEDA supports these efforts through pre-harvest testing, certification programmes such as SHAPHARI, and, together with the Coastal Aquaculture Authority (CAA) and EIC, implementing the National Residue Control Plan (NRCP) to strengthen residue monitoring, traceability, and compliance with international standards. The Department of Fisheries, Government of India, supports quality enhancement and export competitiveness through the PMMSY by promoting branding, standards and certification, capacity building, post-harvest infrastructure, cold-chain facilities, and the modernization of fishing harbours and fish landing centres. The Department of Fisheries, GoIalso supports the culture of export-oriented species such as Scampi, Mud Crab, Asian Seabass, and Cobia, and promotes advanced aquaculture technologies, including Recirculating Aquaculture Systems (RAS) and Biofloc technology. Further, the Department of Fisheries, GoI is promoting sustainable seafood production by supporting the Marine Mammal Stock Assessment Project and the fitment of Turtle Excluder Devices (TEDs) on shrimp trawls under PMMSY. In addition, the CAA has updated the list of prohibited pharmacologically active substances and antimicrobial agents under the Coastal Aquaculture Authority Rules, 2024, to strengthen compliance with international food safety requirements.

The Department of Fisheries, Government of India, has released the National Framework on Traceability in Fisheries and Aquaculture, 2025, to establish a national digital traceability system across the fisheries and aquaculture value chain. The Framework aims to ensure compliance with domestic and international requirements, strengthen food safety, quality assurance, and transparency, enhance consumer confidence, and facilitate market access, thereby boosting seafood exports. It envisages a phased and technology-driven implementation through a National Level Governance Committee (NLGC) for strategic oversight and an Adoption Committee to facilitate the adoption of an IT-based national traceability system by fisheries and aquaculture stakeholders.

Review of implementation of PMMSY

The Department of Fisheries, Government of India (DoF, GoI), regularly reviews the implementation of projects approved under Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) in all States/Union Territories(UTs) including Maharashtra through physical meetings, virtual meetings, field visits and digital monitoring through a dedicated centralized digital platform for scheme implementation.

(b) and (c): Under the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY), during 2020-21 to 2026-27, the DoF, GoI has approved the proposals for fisheries development submitted by the Government of Maharashtra amounting to Rs. 1,601.44 crore, with central share of Rs. 593.33 crore. Out of this, central funds amounting to Rs. 419.02 crore has been released and the Government of Maharashtra has utilized Rs. 408.77 crore of this released funds.

The approvals include various fisheries activities towards enhancing fish production, productivity, strengthening infrastructure facilities, development of cold chain and marketing facilities, sustainability of resources and activities towards fishermen safety and welfare. Under PMMSY, beneficiary selection and projects implementation are done by the respective State Governments/UTs. The district-wise details of important fisheries infrastructure projects implemented under PMMSY as reported by Government of Maharashtra are Annexed.

(d): The Government of Maharashtra has informed total of 3714 beneficiary oriented projects are active in the State under PMMSY out of which 2856 projects are 100% completed. From these, direct employment for 15,763 persons and indirect employment opportunities for 19,528 persons are generated in the State. It is also informed that upon completion of all approved projects, the ongoing projects are expected to generate additional employment opportunities and further strengthen the socio-economic status of the fisheries sector in Maharashtra.

headline: Fisheries, reservoirs and Amrit Sarovars
fullText:
Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying

Fisheries, reservoirs and Amrit Sarovars

22 JUL 2026 1:27PM by PIB Delhi

The Government of India in the Union Budget, 2026-27 has announced the development of fisheries and aquaculture by undertaking integrated development of 500 reservoirs and Amrit Sarovars in the country. The initiative reinforces the Government’s objective to harvest the untapped fisheries potential from reservoirs and Amrit Sarovars, strengthening livelihood of fishermen in the reservoirs and engagement of women self help groups (SHGs), fisheries cooperatives, Fish Farmers Producer Organizations (FFPO) for development of fisheries in Amrit Sarovars and Reservoirs. The key components envisaged for implementation of such initiatives includes strengthening quality seed production by establishing hatchery, promotion of cage and pen culture in reservoir, providing boats and nets to reservoir fishermen, promotion of composite fish culture and integrated fish farming in the Amrit Sarovar, establishment of fisheries infrastructure such as fish feed mill, cold storage, ice plant, fish kiosk and retail fish market, fish transport vehicle and fish value added unit.

(b): The Department of Fisheries (DoF), Government of India (GoI) in consultation with States and Union Territories (UTs) has selected 351 reservoirs and 100 Amrit Sarovars. The selection was based on the lists submitted by the States and UTs, considering the perennial water availability and fisheries potential of the identified reservoirs and Amrit Sarovars.

(c): The Department of Fisheries (DoF), Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying (MoFAH&D), Government of India (GoI) with the objective of enhancing fisher’s income in the country is promoting Fish Farmer Producer Organization (FFPOs), women-led groups and fisheries cooperatives under the Integrated development of Reservoirs and Amrit Sarovars. This includes the formation of new Fish Farmer Producer Organizations (FFPOs), women-led groups, and fisheries cooperatives, as well as support for existing ones, in the selected reservoirs and Amrit Sarovars.

(d): The investment for the implementation of the Integrated Development of Reservoirs and Amrit Sarovars shall be based on the Detailed Project Reports (DPRs) submitted by the States and Union Territories (UTs). The unit cost and funding pattern shall be as per the existing guidelines of the Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY).

This information was given by Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying Minister Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh alias Lalan Singh in the Rajya Sabha in response to a question asked.

(Release ID: 2287584)

The Union Budget 2026-27 outlines an initiative to develop fisheries and aquaculture through the integrated development of 500 reservoirs and Amrit Sarovars across India, focusing on enhancing fishermen livelihoods and women-led group participation.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Selection**: 351 reservoirs and 100 Amrit Sarovars have been chosen by the Department of Fisheries (DoF) based on water availability and fisheries potential from state/UT submissions.  
- **Components**: Includes hatchery establishment, cage/pen culture promotion, provision of boats/nets for fishermen, composite fish culture, and infrastructure like feed mills, cold storage, and retail markets.  
- **Stakeholder Support**: Promotion of Fish Farmer Producer Organizations (FFPOs), women-led groups, and fisheries cooperatives in selected sites.

headline: Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) and Nalanda University Sign MoU to Advance Science-Led Sustainable Development, Technology Outreach and Rural Transformation
fullText:
Ministry of Science & Technology

Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) and Nalanda University Sign MoU to Advance Science-Led Sustainable Development, Technology Outreach and Rural Transformation

22 JUL 2026 1:25PM by PIB Delhi

In a significant step towards integrating science, sustainability and societal development with global higher education, the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) and Nalanda University (NU) today signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to jointly promote technology deployment, sustainability, rural development, skill enhancement and scientific outreach. The partnership aims to leverage CSIR's indigenous technologies and Nalanda University's international academic ecosystem to create scalable models of community-centric development while enhancing the global visibility of Indian scientific innovations.

The MoU was exchanged in the presence of Dr. N. Kalaiselvi, Director General, CSIR & Secretary, DSIR; Prof. Sachin Chaturvedi, Vice-Chancellor, Nalanda University, and Shri Rudrendra Tandon, Secretary (East), Ministry of External Affairs; and along with senior officials, scientists and faculty members from both institutions.

Speaking on the occasion, Prof. Sachin Chaturvedi, Vice-Chancellor, Nalanda University, emphasized the importance of scientific social responsibility, observing that while developing technologies is important, identifying those most relevant to society is an equally significant challenge. He said the collaboration with CSIR would advance sustainable development in and around the Nalanda region through deployment of technologies in areas such as model village development, solid waste management, water quality improvement and environmental sustainability, while strengthening the University's engagement with local communities.

Shri Rudrendra Tandon, Secretary (East), Ministry of External Affairs, said that the Hon'ble Prime Minister envisions Nalanda University as a world-class international institution with a vibrant global network of academic and research partnerships. He noted that collaboration with CSIR, India's premier scientific research organisation, would significantly contribute towards realizing this vision. Describing it as one of Nalanda University's most meaningful partnerships, he expressed confidence that the University's students, researchers and surrounding communities would greatly benefit from CSIR's scientific expertise and innovation ecosystem.

Addressing the gathering, Dr. N. Kalaiselvi, Director General, CSIR & Secretary, DSIR, described the occasion as a landmark in the journey of both institutions and appreciated the collective efforts that culminated in the signing of the MoU. She observed that Nalanda University, with its diverse international student community, provides a unique platform for showcasing India's scientific excellence and expanding the global outreach of CSIR technologies.

Highlighting the importance of interdisciplinary approaches, Dr. Kalaiselvi underscored the critical role of social sciences in ensuring the successful adoption of scientific innovations for societal benefit. She highlighted several CSIR mission-mode technologies and initiatives—including solid waste management, soil reclamation, water quality improvement and societal missions—that can be readily implemented at Nalanda University and in surrounding villages. The DG, CSIR proposed taking up at least three focused societal missions during the initial phase of the collaboration to demonstrate measurable impact in the region.

The MoU envisages establishment of a CSIR Technology Gallery at the Nalanda University campus to showcase indigenous technologies developed by CSIR laboratories for students, researchers and international visitors. It also provides a framework for collaboration in sustainable technology deployment, capacity building, rural outreach and knowledge exchange, creating a replicable model of science-led interventions for societal benefit.

The ceremony concluded with a vote of thanks delivered by the Head, Technology Management Directorate (TMD), CSIR.

headline: Government Strengthens Safeguards Against Digital Addiction; 11.37L+ Citizens Reached Through 6,650 Cyber Awareness Workshops
fullText:
Ministry of Electronics & IT

Government Strengthens Safeguards Against Digital Addiction; 11.37L+ Citizens Reached Through 6,650 Cyber Awareness Workshops

50 OTT Platforms Disabled in Last 2 Years for Obscene Content Under Sections 67 & 67A of IT Act and Other Legal Provisions

Govt Reinforces Safe Digital Ecosystem for Children through Stronger Online Safety, Privacy Protection and Cyber Awareness Measures

22 JUL 2026 1:16PM by PIB Delhi

The policies of the Government are aimed at ensuring an Open, Safe, Trusted and Accountable Internet for its users, including children.

Legal structure:

The Information Technology Act, 2000 (“IT Act”) and the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021 (“IT Rules”), together, have established a framework to promote digital well-being, user safeguards and responsible digital participation.

Obligations of intermediaries:

The IT Rules require that the intermediaries observe due diligence while discharging their duties and shall inform the users of computer resources not to host, display, upload, modify, publish, transmit, update or share any information that is harmful to children or violates any law for the time being in force.

Recent amendments in IT Rules to ensure citizen safety:

Recent amendments to IT Rules require social media platforms and other intermediaries to remove unlawful content within three hours of the receipt of an order of a court of competent jurisdiction or reasoned intimation by the Appropriate Government or its agency.

Data Protection:

Additionally, the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (“DPDP Act”) has been enacted which establishes the legal framework to regulate the processing of digital personal data. The DPDP Act provides a legal framework for safeguarding children’s privacy online.

It mandates parental consent for processing children’s personal data and prohibits practices detrimental to their well-being such as tracking, behavioural monitoring or targeted advertising towards children.

Prohibiting online money gaming:

The Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Act, 2025, represents a major step to address digital addiction and financial harm among youth.

Blocking harmful content:

Section 79(3) (b) of IT Act, 2000 provides for notification to the intermediaries for removing/disabling access to unlawful content. Based on complaints, the Government regularly takes action against such intermediaries and OTT Platforms. The government has disabled 50 OTT Platforms for public access in India in the last two years for displaying obscene content and for violation of Section 67 and 67A of the IT Act, Section 294 of the BNS and Section 4 of the Indecent Representation of Women (Prohibition) Act, 1986.

Digital addiction:

The Economic Survey 2025-26 mentions digital addiction as a serious public health challenge affecting India's children and youth, particularly those aged 15-29 who enjoy near-universal smartphone and internet access.

The Survey further highlights its profound impacts on cognitive development, academic performance, workplace productivity, social connectedness, and mental health, manifesting as anxiety, depression, sleep disorders, cyber bullying, and financial losses from compulsive gaming, social media, and online gambling.

Awareness campaigns:

The government is implementing a project on ‘Information Security Education and Awareness (ISEA)’ for generating human resources in Information Security and creating general awareness on various aspects of cyber hygiene & cyber security among the masses.

So far, 6,650 awareness workshops have been conducted across the country covering 11.37 lakh+ participants, including school/colleges students, teachers, law enforcement, government personnel, and the general public.

Further, multilingual awareness material in the form of handbooks, short videos, posters, brochures, cartoon stories for children, etc. published and disseminated through print, electronic, social media and www.isea.gov.in & https://staysafeonline.in/.

CERT-In also regularly shares safety and security tips and awareness posters, info-graphics and videos on its official websites and social media handles and is aimed at sensitizing internet users on cyber security attacks and frauds including online safety measures for children.

The Ministry of Education issued the PRAGYATA Guidelines on Digital Education in July 2020, which provide a framework for safe and effective online learning, including promotion of students’ well-being and responsible use of social media and electronic devices.

CBSE has supplemented these efforts through guidelines on digital etiquette, cyber-security training for teachers, publication of the ‘Cyber Security Handbook’, and advisories to schools for establishing Cyber Clubs to promote cyber safety awareness.

NCERT has also incorporated cyber safety in its curriculum, including a chapter on “Societal Impacts” in Classes XI and XII, and CIET-NCERT has developed and disseminated resource materials on cyber safety.

This information was submitted by Union Minister for Electronics and Information Technology Shri Ashwini Vaishnaw in Lok Sabha on 22.07.2026.

Dharmendra Tewari / Nayan Solanki / Kanishk Sharma / Vanshita Jaiswal

(Release ID: 2287579)

headline: India Emerges as Global Leader in Railway Electrification with 99.6% Broad Gauge Network Electrified, Second Only to Switzerland
fullText:
Ministry of Railways

India Emerges as Global Leader in Railway Electrification with 99.6% Broad Gauge Network Electrified, Second Only to Switzerland

Diesel Consumption for Railway Traction Falls from 293 Crore Litres in 2015-16 to 108 Crore Litres in 2024-25, Saving Foreign Exchange and Reducing Carbon Emissions

Indian Railways' Electrification Drive Promotes Cleaner Mobility, as Rail Transport Emits About 89% Less CO₂ than Road Transport

Over 1,260 MW of Solar and Wind Power Capacity Commissioned to Support Low-Carbon Operations on Indian Railways

22 JUL 2026 1:01PM by PIB Delhi

Electrification of railway network on Indian Railways has been taken up in mission mode. So far, about 99.6% of Broad Gauge (BG) network has been electrified. The electrification in remaining network has been taken up. Electrification carried out during 2014-26 and before 2014 is as under:

Period | Route Kilometer

Before 2014 (about 60 years) | 21,801

2014-26 | 48,072

Further, all new line / multi-tracking projects are being sanctioned and constructed with electrification.

Indian Railways (IR) has undertaken one of the fastest railway electrification programs in the world. While many countries prioritize electrifying only their busiest passenger and freight corridors, India has chosen to electrify its entire broad gauge network. Indian Railways’ achievement in railway electrification stand out globally. According to the latest report of International Union of Railways (UIC) of June, 2025, railway electrification in important railway systems is as under:

Country | Railway Electrification

Switzerland | 100%

United Kingdom | 39%

By network coverage, Indian Railways is now among the most extensively electrified large railway systems in the world. Indian Railways had electrified 99.6% of its broad-gauge network, completing one of the world’s largest infrastructure transformation programs in a decade.

Electric traction is not only environmental friendly but energy efficient also. Reduction in CO2 emissions in transportation by railways as compared to transportation by road is as under (Ref:- NITI Aayog Report titled “Fast Tracking Freight In India”, June 2021)

Mode of Transportation | CO2 emission for transportation of 1 tonne for 1 km

Rail | 11.5 gm (about 89% less)

Further, electrification of railway tracks reduces dependency on fossil fuels, decreased diesel consumption resulting in lower carbon emission. Electrification enables better haulage capacity and higher train speeds, leading to reduced travel time and enhanced efficiency.

Reduction in IR’s diesel consumption for traction purpose by 185 crore litres with about 108 crore litres consumption in 2024-25 as compared to 293 crore litres in 2015-16. This has resulted in saving of precious foreign exchange with Government of India exchequer, which would have otherwise been needed for import of fuel which has been saved due to electrification.

Electrified railways are well suited for integrating solar, wind & other renewable energy into traction power systems, making them most effective transport systems for utilizing renewable electricity. Indian Railways is committed to sustainable operations through near total Railway Electrification combined with use of renewable energy sources, based on strategic power procurement planning, thus contributing to carbon footprint reduction.

Indian Railways aims to progressively meet its electric traction power requirements through renewable energy sources including solar, wind and hybrid systems (solar, wind and energy storage) thereby reducing carbon footprints of IR. As of June, 2026 about 1161 Mega Watt (MW) of solar plants (both on Rooftops and on land) and about 103 MW of Wind power plants have been commissioned.

Mission 100% Electrification is a highly challenging project, as it involves electrifying railway lines that are already in operation and encountering unforeseen various challenges during execution. To expedite railway electrification work, various steps have been taken by Indian Railways which include among others; formation of Project Monitoring Group (PMG) portal to resolve the constraints being faced during commissioning, ensuring effective project monitoring mechanism, assured funding and enhanced financial powers to field units.

### OVERVIEW  
The Ministry of Railways announced that Indian Railways has electrified 99.6% of its Broad Gauge (BG) network, positioning it as a global leader in railway electrification second only to Switzerland. The initiative has significantly reduced diesel consumption and carbon emissions while integrating renewable energy sources to support sustainable operations.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **Electrification Progress**: 99.6% of the BG network electrified, with 48,072 route kilometers electrified during 2014-26 and 21,801 route kilometers before 2014.  
- **Diesel Reduction**: Diesel consumption for traction fell from 293 crore liters in 2015-16 to 108 crore liters in 2024-25, saving foreign exchange and cutting carbon emissions.  
- **Renewable Energy Integration**: Over 1,260 MW of solar and wind power capacity commissioned (1,161 MW solar and 103 MW wind as of June 2026) to power electrified railways.  
- **Environmental Impact**: Rail transport emits 11.5 gm CO₂ per tonne-km, 89% less than road transport, according to a 2021 NITI Aayog report.  
- **Zone-Wise Electrification**: All 19 railway zones electrified at 96.1%–100%, with Northeast Frontier Railway (98.7%) and Southern Railway (98.7%) at near-complete levels.  
- **State-Wise Electrification**: 26 states/UTs listed, with 24 achieving 100% electrification; exceptions include Rajasthan (99.8%), Tamil Nadu (98.3%), Assam (97.9%), Karnataka (96.8%), and Goa (91.4%).  
- **Mission 100% Electrification**: Accelerated through measures like the Project Monitoring Group (PMG) portal, ensuring funding and enhanced field-level powers.

headline: DAE-Nurtured Olympiad Programme Powers India's Yuva Shakti at International Science and Mathematics Olympiads 2026
fullText:
Department of Atomic Energy

DAE-Nurtured Olympiad Programme Powers India's Yuva Shakti at International Science and Mathematics Olympiads 2026

Indian students delivered exceptional performances across the International Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics Olympiads 2026, reinforcing India's growing stature in global scientific excellence.

The 2026 campaign yielded 12 Gold, and 7 Silver medals, including historic all Golden Sweeps at the International Physics Olympiad and the International Chemistry Olympiad.

The achievements reflect the strength of India's National Olympiad Programme in the sciences and mathematics, spearheaded by the Homi Bhabha Centre for Science Education (HBCSE), a National Centre of the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR), an aided institution under the Department of Atomic Energy (DAE).

For almost four decades, the Department of Atomic Energy has provided the institutional foundation for identifying, mentoring and preparing India's brightest young minds to excel at the world's most prestigious science and mathematics competitions.

22 JUL 2026 11:52AM by PIB Delhi

The Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) has once again reaffirmed its enduring commitment to nurturing India's scientific talent, as the students trained under the National Olympiad Programme delivered one of the country's finest-ever performances at the International Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics Olympiads 2026. The achievements underscore the extraordinary potential of India's youth and reflect the strength of a national ecosystem painstakingly built over decades to identify, mentor and inspire the nation's brightest young minds. The remarkable success of the Olympiad teams is a powerful reflection of India's Yuva Shakti and of the country's sustained investment in creating opportunities for talented young students to realise their full potential on the global stage.

The 2026 Olympiad season stands out as a landmark chapter in India's performance at the International Olympiads. Across the four Olympiads, the Indian contingent won an impressive 12 Gold, 7 Silver, including historic Golden Sweeps in Physics and Chemistry, besides outstanding performances in Biology and Mathematics. Competing against the world's finest young minds, Indian students demonstrated exceptional scientific aptitude, perseverance and innovation, bringing immense pride to the nation and reaffirming India's emergence as a global force in science and mathematics.

International Olympiads in 2026 | India's Performance

International Physics Olympiad | All 5 Gold, Joint World No. 1

International Chemistry Olympiad | All 4 Gold, Joint World No. 1

International Biology Olympiad | 1 Gold, 3 Silver

International Mathematical Olympiad | 2 Gold, 4 Silver

Congratulating the students on their exceptional accomplishments, Dr. Ajit Kumar Mohanty, Secretary, Department of Atomic Energy and Chairman, Atomic Energy Commission, said, “The outstanding performances of Indian students at the International Olympiads are a matter of immense national pride and a celebration of India's scientific spirit. These achievements reflect the dedication of the students, the invaluable guidance of mentors and teachers, and the strength of the institutional ecosystem painstakingly built over decades.”

At the heart of this success is the Homi Bhabha Centre for Science Education (HBCSE), a National Centre of the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR), an aided institution under the Department of Atomic Energy. As the national nodal centre for India's Science and Mathematical Olympiad Programmes, HBCSE conducts a rigorous, multi-stage process of talent identification, orientation, advanced training and mentoring, preparing the country's brightest pre-university students to compete at the highest international level. The programme has evolved into one of the world's most respected Olympiad ecosystems through DAE's sustained institutional support and unwavering commitment to scientific excellence.

Acknowledging the unstinting support extended by DAE in nurturing India's Olympiad programme, Prof. Arnab Bhattacharya, Centre Director, HBCSE said, "We are deeply grateful to the Department of Atomic Energy for its invaluable support to HBCSE and for its long-standing commitment to nurturing scientific talent in the country. The outstanding performances of our students reaffirm our mission of identifying, nurturing and inspiring the next generation of young scholars, while fostering a culture of excellence in science and mathematics across the country."

The Department of Atomic Energy's contribution to the Olympiad movement extends well beyond sponsorship. Through its support to TIFR and HBCSE, DAE has helped create an enduring national ecosystem that brings together scientists, teachers, academic institutions and subject experts from across the country in a shared mission to nurture scientific talent. This collaborative framework has consistently produced generations of Olympians who have gone on to distinguish themselves in higher education, research, innovation and scientific leadership, both in India and abroad.

The Department of Atomic Energy congratulates all the medal winners, team leaders, mentors, scientific observers, the Olympiad Cells at HBCSE, teacher organisations and the nationwide network of resource persons whose dedication has made these achievements possible. DAE also acknowledges the continued partnership of the Department of Science and Technology, Department of Space, and the Ministry of Education, Government of India, whose support has further strengthened India's National Olympiad Programme.

The achievements of India's Olympiad teams in 2026 reaffirm the transformative impact of sustained investment in scientific education and talent nurture. As the nation advances towards becoming a global knowledge economy, the Department of Atomic Energy remains steadfast in its commitment to inspiring young scientific minds, strengthening institutions of excellence, and building a future powered by science, innovation and human potential.

Indian Team at the 56th International Physics Olympiad (IPhO) 2026, held in Bucaramanga, Colombia

Indian Team at the 58th International Chemistry Olympiad, Tashkent, Uzbekistan

Indian Team at the 37th International Biology Olympiad 2026 in Vilnius, Lithuania

Indian Team at the 67th International Mathematical Olympiad in Shanghai, China
